title: Wall St Week Ahead US earnings season set to test war-rattled stocks
author: Sigma Capital

excerpt: US earnings season is expected to show a 14% rise in S&P 500 company earnings, testing war-affected stocks. Major U.S. stock indexes reached record highs, driven by robust first-quarter earnings expectations. Nearly one-fifth of S&P 500 companies are set to report results.

# Wall Street’s week ahead: Surging record-high U.S. stocks to wade deeper into earnings season. Traders work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) at the opening bell in New York on April 16.TIMOTHY A. Investors ​will look to a heavy week of U.S. corporate results to further fuel ‌a stunning rebound in the U.S. stock market, which has shaken off war-related concerns to reach record peaks. Hopes for a cooling of U.S.-Iran tensions have led to a sharp rally this month, culminating with major U.S. stock indexes minting fresh records in recent days. The benchmark S&P 500 on Wednesday posted its first record-high close since Jan 27, while the ⁠Nasdaq Composite ​on the same day notched its first all-time-high close since Oct 29. Investors are turning to a first-quarter earnings season that is expected to be robust, providing a key pillar buttressing bullish sentiment for stocks. Nearly one-fifth of S&P 500 companies are slated to report results in the coming week.
